pages.raw_text:
Making glad the waste places
by means of irrigation is in full
swing in this state. and the result
in a few years will be a land of
fragrance and fruit that will rival
California. An indication of the
volume of irrigated tract invest
ment is furnished in the following
list of recent sales by the Rich
land Land Co., which is handling
a new irrigated land project on
the Columbia river nine miles
above Kennewick:


DR.. Alfred Oliver of Elberton,
Ga. has purchased 60 acres for
$4,000, which he is setting out to
high grade winter apples. John
Brooks of Ross park has purchased
10 acres close to the river bank
and is setting it out to early fruits.
Clark Alexander of Darcy, Idaho,
has just started to improve his 10
acre tract and will plant to cher
ries, peaches and pears. Jens
Sorensen of Spokane, who pur-
chased 10 acres right on the river
bank for $1,000 per acre, is just
starting a fine residence. E. e.
Ostroot and some friends, of Mos
cow, Idaho, have started improve
ments on their tract of 40 acres,
which they bought for $13,75O.
The United Industries Co. of Chi
cago has purchased 1000 acres for
$142,900, on which they are set
ting winter apples, and hundreds
of families will come west in the
next few weeks and settle on five
and 10 acre tracts. E. P. Manor,
Spokane capitalist, has taken SO
acres, which he is having all set
to asparagus, berries and early
fruits. Frank Saunders of Port
land purchased % acres for $1O,-
600 as an investment Thomas T.
Louis of Oklahoma settled on
beautiful 40 acre tract adjoining
the town, for which he paid $1O,-
900.
